creator: swills@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi,

Sorry for the slow reply on this. I looked at this previously but
apparently didn't send my response. Anyway, I looked at this again and
verified that I had the same results as before. For me, it seems that
having the wrapper actually suid isn't necessary. When I start up
vmware, the mouse pointer does not release, so I run
vmware-user-suid-wrapper. This gives an error about vmblock, but doesn't
fix the mouse pointer issue. However, if I run it a second time, it does
fix the mouse issue (although it does again give the error about
vmblock). Can you verify this is the case for you?

Thanks,
Steve
